analysis.summary: The US and Israel launched major joint strikes against Iran beginning February 28, 2026, triggering an escalating conflict now in its 24th day. President Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum demanding Iran reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face strikes on its power plants, while Iran responded with counter-threats including mining the Persian Gulf, striking Gulf power plants, and firing long-range missiles at the joint UK-US base at Diego Garcia. The IEA chief warned the conflict has triggered an energy crisis worse than the 1970s oil shocks, with global markets, Asia, and Europe all experiencing severe supply disruptions.

analysis.summary: The Trump administration announced that ICE agents would be deployed to U.S. airports to assist TSA officers amid ongoing staffing shortages and long security lines caused by a partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security. The move drew both support and criticism, with debates over its legality, practicality, and political framing.
